title: Osteospermum tomentosum
taxonomy: Osteospermum tomentosum is classified as a member of the Asteraceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as sunflowers or daisies. Within the Asteraceae family, Osteospermum tomentosum is further classified as a member of the genus Osteospermum, a group of flowering plants native to Africa and South America. The specific classification of Osteospermum tomentosum is as Osteospermum tomentosum (L.) DC., which indicates its scientific name and the botanist who first described it. This classification system helps to organize and identify the various species within the plant kingdom. Through this classification, botanists and scientists can better understand the relationships between different plant species and their evolutionary history.

intro: Osteospermum tomentosum, also known as the woolly daisy or cotton daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Osteospermum tomentosum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
